title: Retry Safety, Replayability, Backoff, Jitter, and Retry Budgets · Browser and Node Client Systems · Compiler Case: Retry After Rate Status
summary: A high-traffic web SDK retries selected reads under 429 and 503 without replaying one-shot streams or synchronizing every browser tab. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts retry after rate status increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch bounded retry and replay decision engine. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Interpret Retry-After for 429 or 503, bound unreasonable values, and reconcile it with the caller deadline. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Apply capped exponential backoff with an explicit jitter strategy and deterministic test clock. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
